pages.raw_text:
There were some things of in.
terest brought to the surface in the
hearing in Justice Hyde's court
this morning in the cases of the
three union machinists arrested d
for alleged beating up of two
strikebreakers, employed at the
Union Iron works.


First, it appears that Chief of
Police Sullivan and Prosecuting
Attorney Fred c. Pugh, on com
plant of Manager Fitzpatrick of
the Union Iron works. caused war
rants to be issued for e. J. Gra-
ham and Tom Steinbach. union
machnists, when they had no evi-
dence against them. Graham and
Steinbach were confined in the city
Jail for nearly seven hours, after
they walked into the police station
and gave themselves up. on learn
ing that warrants were out for
them. They We're charged with
beating one Halverson, a strike
breaker. This morning, in Justice
Hyde's court, the two men were
dismissed and the case dropped
without asking question. This
case illustrates how much the city
administration and the prosecuting
attorneys office loves organized la
Dor.


The reason that Graham and
Steinbach were wanted is because
they were on the executive com
mittee of the striking machinists
